name: review-depth-supply-chain
author: diffscope
description: Supply-chain and dependency trust benchmark pack for deeper live review runs.

fixtures.name: yaml-unpinned-github-action
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/build.yml b/.github/workflows/build.yml
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/build.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml
@@ -6,4 +6,5 @@ jobs:
   build:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     steps:
-      - uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3

expected_findings.description: actions/checkout is no longer pinned to an immutable commit SHA.
contains_any: pin github action to a commit sha

expected_findings.description: docker/setup-buildx-action is also introduced with a mutable tag instead of a commit SHA.
contains_any: pin github action to a commit sha

fixtures.description: Workflow dependencies should stay pinned to immutable revisions.

fixtures.name: docker-curl-pipe-bash
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/Dockerfile b/Dockerfile
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/Dockerfile
+++ b/Dockerfile
@@ -3,3 +3,4 @@ FROM python:3.12-slim
 RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y curl
-RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
+RUN curl -fsSL https://downloads.example.com/install.sh | bash
+RUN pip install -r requirements.txt

expected_findings.description: Remote installation script is piped directly into a shell without verification.

fixtures.description: Build pipelines should not execute unverified remote scripts.

fixtures.name: yaml-pull-request-target-script-injection
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/pr-check.yml b/.github/workflows/pr-check.yml
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/pr-check.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/pr-check.yml
@@ -1,5 +1,8 @@
 name: PR Check
-on: pull_request
+on: pull_request_target
 jobs:
   test:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - run: echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" | bash

expected_findings.description: pull_request_target can expose write-scoped credentials to untrusted PR code.
Attacker-controlled GitHub event data is piped into bash.
contains_any: pr title is piped directly to bash

fixtures.description: CI workflows should not combine pull_request_target with attacker-controlled shell input.

fixtures.name: npm-typosquat-package
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/package.json b/package.json
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/package.json
+++ b/package.json
@@ -5,5 +5,6 @@
   "dependencies": {
     "express": "^4.19.2",
-    "lodash": "^4.17.21"
+    "lodash": "^4.17.21",
+    "lodahs": "^4.17.21"
   }
 }

expected_findings.description: New dependency name looks like a typosquat of lodash.

fixtures.description: Review newly added dependencies for typo-squatting and other supply-chain red flags.

fixtures.name: python-unpinned-dependency
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/requirements.txt b/requirements.txt
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/requirements.txt
+++ b/requirements.txt
@@ -1,2 +1,3 @@
 flask==3.0.3
 sqlalchemy==2.0.36
+requests

expected_findings.description: Dependency is added without a pinned version.

fixtures.description: Requirements files should pin newly introduced packages to exact or tightly controlled versions.

fixtures.name: rust-yanked-crate-version
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/Cargo.toml b/Cargo.toml
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/Cargo.toml
+++ b/Cargo.toml
@@ -8,5 +8,5 @@ edition = "2021"
 [dependencies]
 anyhow = "1"
-time = "0.3.36"
+time = "=0.1.43" # downgrade to an old yanked release from a previous incident postmortem
 tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }

expected_findings.description: Dependency is downgraded to an old yanked or known-bad crate version.

fixtures.description: Dependency downgrades to suspiciously old or yanked releases should be treated as supply-chain risk.

fixtures.name: go-replace-directive-remote
fixtures.diff_content:
diff --git a/go.mod b/go.mod
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/go.mod
+++ b/go.mod
@@ -3,3 +3,4 @@ module example.com/payments
 go 1.22
 require github.com/gorilla/mux v1.8.1
+replace github.com/gorilla/mux => corp.example.com/forks/mux v1.8.1

expected_findings.description: go.mod replace directive redirects module resolution to a non-standard remote source.

fixtures.description: Module replacement directives should be scrutinized because they bypass the normal upstream source of a dependency.
